items.headline: U.S. Markets Closed for Weekend Amid Geopolitical Tensions and Inflation Watch
items.summary: U.S. stock markets are closed today, Saturday, April 4, 2026. Trading activity for the week concluded on Thursday, April 2, with Friday being observed as Good Friday. The past week saw the S&P 500 achieve its best weekly gain in four months, despite an underlying sentiment influenced by persistent geopolitical tensions in the Middle East and concerns over sticky inflation. Investors are re-evaluating risks following a strong U.S. labor market report for March, which continues to shape expectations for Federal Reserve policy, while oil prices remain elevated.

sections.title: Top Movers (Latest Available US Market Data)

items.headline: Intel Soars on Sector Sentiment
items.summary: Intel Corporation saw a significant jump on Thursday, April 2nd, rising on strong semiconductor sector sentiment.

items.headline: Persistent Systems Ltd (India) Jumps
items.summary: Persistent Systems Ltd, an Indian IT company, recorded a strong performance on April 4th, reflecting positive movement in its local market.

items.headline: Tesla Plunges on Delivery Concerns
items.summary: Tesla, Inc. experienced a notable decline on Thursday, April 2nd, after its first-quarter deliveries fell short of estimates.

items.headline: GE Aerospace Weighs on Industrials
items.summary: GE Aerospace's shares dropped on Thursday, April 2nd, contributing negatively to the industrial average.

items.headline: Federal Reserve Expected to Hold Rates in April Meeting
items.summary: Prediction markets indicate a high probability that the Federal Reserve will not change interest rates after its April 2026 meeting. This follows the release of a strong U.S. labor market report for March, which could maintain high government bond yields and reduce the likelihood of rapid easing by the Fed.

items.headline: Sticky Inflation and Geopolitical Tensions Drive Oil Prices Higher
items.summary: Market participants are contending with sticky inflation and rising bond yields. Geopolitical tensions, particularly the ongoing war in Iran, are driving oil prices higher, with U.S. crude trading around $111.54 per barrel. European ministers are even calling for profit caps on energy companies due to surging prices.

items.headline: Technology Sector Faces Headwinds Amid AI Concerns
items.summary: While the S&P 500 has seen a recent rally, the technology sector has lagged year-to-date. Concerns about artificial intelligence's impact on software and broader technology business models, as well as geopolitical tensions, are creating headwinds for semiconductor stocks. Capital is reportedly rotating out of overvalued software into more tangible assets.

items.headline: Energy, Materials, and Staples Lead YTD Performance
items.summary: For the year-to-date, Energy, Materials, and Consumer Staples sectors have been the best-performing. Utilities are also expected to benefit from increased demand from data centers and infrastructure updates.

items.headline: Major IT Earnings on the Horizon for Next Week
items.summary: India's top IT companies, including TCS, Infosys, Wipro, HCL Technologies, and Tech Mahindra, are scheduled to release their Q4 FY26 earnings reports starting next week. Meanwhile, Meta Platforms' next earnings report is anticipated around April 28th.

items.headline: U.S. Private Sector Activity Slows in March
items.summary: U.S. private-sector activity experienced a marked slowdown in March, with growth nearly stalling. This downturn was primarily driven by a contraction in services activity and weakening overall demand conditions.
